calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-26-2021 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-12-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF 665 THIRD STREET ASSOCIATES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is OFF CALENDAR, dismissal on file.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Production From Oto Analytics, Inc.
Off Calendar Aug-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DANA M. ANDREOLI, Esq. of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP (Tel: 415-421-3400) for plaintiff;
Atty. CONRAD V. SISON, Esq. of Scale LLP (Tel: 415-735-5933) for defendant Oto Analytics, Inc. dba Womply.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF 665 THIRD STREET ASSOCIATES's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Production From Oto Analytics, Inc.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications, as follows:

Plaintiff's motion to compel Defendant's further responses to Special Interrogatories,
Set One, Nos. 7, 8, 10, and 11 is GRANTED, as the Court finds that the requested information
is relevant.
Plaintiff's motion to compel Defendant's further responses to Request for Production of
Documents, Set Two, No. 9 is GRANTED as to the documents identified in or relied on by
Defendant in its amended responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One, Nos. 7, 8, 10 and 11.
Plaintiff's motion to compel Defendant's further responses to Request for Production of
Documents, Set Two, No. 10 is GRANTED as to Defendant's financial statements for October
2019 to the present, as they are relevant and placed at issue by Section 11.05 of the subject
lease. Plaintiff's motion to compel as to the balance of Request for Production of Documents,
Set Two, No. 10, is DENIED as both overbroad and compound.
Defendant shall serve verified amended responses and produce responsive documents, as
applicable, to Plaintiff no later than ten (10) Court days from notice of entry of this Order.
Plaintiff's motion to compel is DENIED as to Special Interrogatories, Set One, Nos. 1,
2, 3, 4, 5 and 6 as the interrogatories are vague, ambiguous and overbroad.
Plaintiff's motion to compel is DENIED as to Special Interrogatories, Set One, Nos. 9
and 12, as there was no foundational showing that a lease in Utah is relevant to the subject lease in San Francisco, CA.
Plaintiff's motion to compel is DENIED as to Requests for Production of Documents,
Set Two, Nos. 6, 7 and 8 as the requests are overbroad.
Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED.
This Order is without prejudice to the parties' rights to conduct further discovery as
allowed by code.

The moving party is to prepare a proposed order in editable MS Word format and e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at maulawsf@hotmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Michael Mau; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Production From Oto Analytics, Inc. (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Aug-04-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Waiver; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities;
Off Calendar Aug-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DANA M. ANDREOLI, Esq. of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP (Tel: 415-421-3400) for plaintiff;
Atty. CONRAD V. SISON, Esq. of Scale LLP (Tel: 415-735-5933) for defendant Oto Analytics, Inc. dba Womply.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT OTO ANALYTICS, INC. DBA WOMPLY's Motion For Relief From Waiver.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's Motion for Relief from Waiver of its objections pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Sections 2030.290(a)(interrogatories) and 2031.300(a)(demands for inspection) is granted.

The moving party is to prepare a proposed order in editable MS Word format and e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at maulawsf@hotmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Michael Mau; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Aug-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DANA M. ANDREOLI, Esq. of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP (Tel: 415-421-3400) for plaintiff;
Atty. CONRAD V. SISON, Esq. of Scale LLP (Tel: 415-735-5933) for defendant Oto Analytics, Inc. dba Womply.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT OTO ANALYTICS, INC. DBA WOMPLY's Motion For Protective Order.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Motion for a Protective Order is denied in part and granted in part. The Motion is denied in part due to it being moot, as a result of the Court's ruling in the preceding Motion to Compel Discovery Responses between these same parties. However, the Motion is granted in part, wherein the Court Orders that the parties Discovery answers and responsive documents produced in this case may be marked "Confidential" or "Highly Confidential - Attorney's Eyes Only" and that such marked records shall not be publicly disseminated but are subject to full use in this litigation.

No sanctions are awarded.

The moving party is to prepare a proposed order in editable MS Word format and e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at maulawsf@hotmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Michael Mau; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
